channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: “Technology is our superpower”—Africa and India take seat at top table as AI revolution spreads
item.description: Emerging economies like Nigeria and India are leapfrogging into a tech-driven future, where mobile networks and AI could redefine development—and resh...

item.title: The last 3 years were the hottest ever recorded. Here’s why we may look back at them as some of the coolest we remember
item.description: By most indicators, the planet should have been cooler in 2025 than it was.

item.title: Kristi Noem’s Firing, and the Boom of American Billionaires
We’re economists who designed a chatbot to help our students reason instead of cheat. Meet ‘Macro Buddy’
item.description: About 90% of 1,100 U.S. students surveyed at two-year and four-year colleges in 2025 reported using generative AI.

item.title: Why the Pentagon Wants to Destroy Anthropic
item.description: The former A.I. policy adviser to the Trump White House explains why the conflict between Anthropic and the White House is so dangerous.

item.title: $3.7 billion whisper: the explosive growth of quiet corporate activism
item.description: Sometimes, silence speaks volumes. There's a quiet revolution taking place in corporate purpose.

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

item.title: Indonesia to Block Children Under 16 From Social Media
item.description: The ban is to take effect March 28, according to a government minister, but details about how it would be implemented were scarce.

item.title: The President is surprised by the lack of European support for military action against Iran—he shouldn’t be
item.description: With Iraq’s legacy still lingering and a changing America in view, Europe is no longer willing to fall in line behind Washington’s wars—even when alli...

item.title: Live Updates: Israel Pummels Beirut, Intensifying Strikes on Hezbollah
item.description: Thousands fled the heaviest Israeli attacks on Lebanon’s capital since a 2024 war with the Iran-backed militia. Israel also said it was hitting Tehran...

item.title: 180,000 New Yorkers May Lose Food Stamp Benefits Under New Work Rules
item.description: Social workers are scrambling to alert recipients and help them find jobs before their aid is eliminated under President Trump’s sweeping domestic pol...

item.title: Indonesia to Block Children Under 16 From Social Media
item.description: The ban is to take effect March 28, according to a government minister, but details about how it would be implemented were scarce.

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

item.title: 4 Arrested in London on Suspicion of Spying on Jews for Iran
item.description: The police said one Iranian and three British-Iranians were detained as part of a counterterrorism investigation.
